steps.title: Identify the decisive signal
steps.content: The passage states Lear “expresses regret for his actions”—the quotation must show him blaming himself for his own mistakes, not self-pity, avoidance, or threats.

steps.title: Find the quotation of self-blame
steps.content: C directly shows Lear criticizing himself—”thy folly” and his lost “judgement” are his own failures. The other three shift blame outward, evade accountability, or express threats instead. That matches choice C — C is correct.
